[MrBot] Replace PowerShell repo validation with compiled Rust validator (63x faster) (#353)

* Add compiled repo_validator core infrastructure

Introduce a single C executable (repo_validator) that replaces PowerShell
validation scripts with a compiled tool for significantly faster execution.

Core components:
- repo_validator.h: Common types, check interface, file classification
- main.c: CLI parsing, check registration, orchestration
- file_walker.c: Cross-platform directory traversal (Windows + Linux)
- CMakeLists.txt: Build configuration for the executable

The tool supports:
- --repo-root, --exclude-folders, --fix, --check <name> flags
- Single directory traversal shared across all active checks
- PowerShell-style argument compatibility (-RepoRoot, -Fix, etc.)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Phase 1 compiled checks: no_tabs, file_endings, requirements_naming, srs_uniqueness

Simple validators migrated from PowerShell to C:
- check_no_tabs: Detects tab characters, replaces with 4 spaces in fix mode
- check_file_endings: Validates CRLF line endings, appends in fix mode
- check_requirements_naming: Enforces _requirements.md suffix in devdoc
- check_srs_uniqueness: Detects duplicate SRS tags across markdown files

Benchmark on ebs repo (6,612 files):
  PowerShell (4 scripts sequential): 25.9s
  C tool (single traversal):          1.5s  (17x faster)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Phase 2 compiled checks: enable_mocks, no_vld_include, no_backticks, test_spec_tags

Moderate-complexity validators migrated from PowerShell to C:
- check_enable_mocks: Flags deprecated #define/#undef ENABLE_MOCKS,
  replaces with include pattern, respects // force exemption
- check_no_vld_include: Detects #include vld.h, removes standalone
  includes and #ifdef USE_VLD blocks in fix mode
- check_no_backticks_in_srs: Strips markdown backticks from SRS text
- check_test_spec_tags: Validates TEST_FUNCTION() has preceding
  Tests_*_DD_DDD spec tags, supports // no-srs exemption

Uses pre-built line index for O(1) line access (vs O(n) rescan).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove 8 migrated PowerShell validation scripts

These scripts have been replaced by the compiled repo_validator:
- validate_no_tabs.ps1
- validate_file_endings.ps1
- validate_requirements_naming.ps1
- validate_srs_uniqueness.ps1
- validate_enable_mocks_pattern.ps1
- validate_no_vld_include.ps1
- validate_no_backticks_in_srs.ps1
- validate_test_spec_tags.ps1

Only 2 complex scripts remain (Phase 3 candidates):
- validate_aaa_comments.ps1
- validate_srs_consistency.ps1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update CMake integration and tests for compiled validator

- repo_validation/CMakeLists.txt: Build repo_validator exe when
  run_repo_validation=ON, route 8 migrated checks to C tool,
  remaining 2 checks still use PowerShell
- All 8 test CMakeLists.txt updated to invoke repo_validator
  instead of powershell scripts
- Tests use DEPENDS repo_validator for build ordering

All 8 test suites pass (clean + fix), all 8 detection tests
correctly identify violations.

Benchmark (ebs repo, 6,612 files, 18,356 TEST_FUNCTIONs):
  8 PowerShell scripts (sequential): ~70s
  Compiled C tool (single traversal):  1.8s  (39x faster)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply coding conventions to repo_validator source files

Enforce project coding standards across all C source files:
- Single return statement per function (no early returns)
- Every if has an explicit else (with /* do nothing */ where needed)
- Error path in if, success path in else
- (void) cast on all ignored return values (printf, memcpy, fwrite, etc.)
- Allman brace style throughout
- result variable pattern for function return values
- Consistent if/else error handling chains

No behavioral changes - only style/convention fixes.
All 8 test suites verified passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Rust implementation of repo_validator for comparison

Parallel Rust implementation (src_rust/) of the same 8 validation checks
using only stdlib (no external crates), targeting ms-prod-1.93 toolchain.

Files: 12 source files (main.rs, config.rs, file_walker.rs, 8 checks + mod.rs)

Benchmark comparison on ebs repo (6,612 files, 8 checks):
  PowerShell (original):  ~70s
  C implementation:       1.62s  (43x faster)
  Rust implementation:    1.12s  (62x faster, 1.4x faster than C)

Code size comparison:
  C:    11 files, 6,144 lines
  Rust: 12 files, 3,815 lines  (38% less code)

Both C and Rust implementations pass all 16 test cases
(8 clean + 8 detection) and produce identical results on ebs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add CMake integration for Rust validator build and tests

- src_rust/CMakeLists.txt: Builds repo_validator_rs via cargo using
  add_custom_command with stamp file for incremental builds
- tests_rust/CMakeLists.txt: 8 test suites (clean + fix) reusing the
  same test fixture directories as the C tests (no file duplication)
- repo_validation/CMakeLists.txt: New option run_repo_validation_rust=ON
  to enable the Rust build/tests alongside the C version

Usage:
  cmake -Drun_repo_validation=ON -Drun_repo_validation_rust=ON ...
  cmake --build . --target test_validate_all_rs  # run all Rust tests
  cmake --build . --target test_validate_no_tabs_rs  # individual check

Build time comparison (clean build):
  C (CMake generate + MSVC):  6.9s (5.5s gen + 1.4s compile)
  Rust (cargo --release):     0.9s
  Incremental (1 file): C=0.8s, Rust=0.9s (comparable)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add PARAMETERIZED_TEST_FUNCTION support to C and Rust validators

Port the change from PR #361 (745bce2) that added
PARAMETERIZED_TEST_FUNCTION detection to validate_test_spec_tags.ps1.

Both C and Rust implementations now:
- Detect PARAMETERIZED_TEST_FUNCTION declarations in addition to
  TEST_FUNCTION
- Extract the test name up to the first comma (not closing paren)
  for PARAMETERIZED_TEST_FUNCTION
- Report the correct macro name in error output

Resolved merge conflict from rebase onto master where the PS script
was modified (745bce2) after our branch deleted it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add check_aaa_comments and check_srs_consistency C validation checks

Implement two new repo_validator checks ported from Rust reference:

- check_aaa_comments: Validates _ut.c test functions contain AAA
  (Arrange/Act/Assert) comments. Supports TEST_FUNCTION, TEST_METHOD,
  CTEST_FUNCTION, PARAMETERIZED_TEST_FUNCTION macros. Checks helper
  functions for delegated AAA markers. Supports no-aaa exemption.

- check_srs_consistency: Validates SRS requirement text matches between
  devdoc markdown files and C code comments. Handles block comments,
  incomplete block comments, and line comments. Strips markdown formatting
  (bold, italics, backticks, escapes). Checks tag placement (Codes_SRS_
  in prod files, Tests_SRS_ in test files). Supports fix mode.

Registration: Added both checks to repo_validator.h, main.c, src/CMakeLists.txt.
CMake integration: Added to C_CHECKS list and MIGRATED_SCRIPTS in CMakeLists.txt.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Complete Phase 3: migrate aaa_comments and srs_consistency to C and Rust

All 10 validation checks are now implemented in both C and Rust.
Only validate_c_build_tools_ref.ps1 remains as PowerShell (added in
PR #354, not in scope for this migration).

New files:
- src/checks/check_aaa_comments.c: AAA comment validation with
  brace-balanced function body extraction, string/char literal
  skipping, and helper function delegation
- src/checks/check_srs_consistency.c: Cross-file SRS tag consistency
  between markdown and C comments, tag placement validation,
  markdown formatting normalization
- src_rust/src/checks/aaa_comments.rs: Rust equivalent
- src_rust/src/checks/srs_consistency.rs: Rust equivalent

Updated:
- Test CMakeLists for both checks (PS -> repo_validator)
- Rust test targets for both new checks
- CMake integration (C_CHECKS, MIGRATED_SCRIPTS lists)

Deleted: validate_aaa_comments.ps1, validate_srs_consistency.ps1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Rust srs_consistency markdown parser for alternate bold format

Handle markdown SRS tags where bold closes before the colon:
  **SRS_TAG:** [** text **]**  (colon after bold close)
in addition to the standard format:
  **SRS_TAG: [** text **]**  (colon inside bold)

Skip bold markers (**) and whitespace between colon and opening
bracket, matching the C implementation's behavior.

Fixes test fixture detection (consistent/inconsistent fixtures
use the alternate format) and maintains ebs repo parity at
24,670 markdown requirements for both C and Rust.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add srs_format check to detect malformed SRS markdown tags

New validation check that catches malformed SRS requirement tags in
devdoc markdown files — the exact issues fixed in ebs PR #15288373.

Detects:
- C-comment-style closings: ]*/ instead of **]**
- Missing trailing bold: **] instead of **]**
- Missing bold opening bracket: [ instead of [**
- Other malformed closings: ]*), **]*, etc.

Implemented in both C (check_srs_format.c, 255 lines) and Rust
(srs_format.rs, 127 lines) with identical output.

Includes test fixtures with 6 violation patterns and 5 clean tags.
Both C and Rust CMake test suites pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review comments

- file_walker.rs: Add doc comment explaining u32 bitmask design choice
- file_walker.rs:79: Add comment explaining let-match error skip
- file_walker.rs:173: Add comment noting silent skip matches PS behavior
- aaa_comments.rs: Replace magic numbers with .len() references
- tests_rust/CMakeLists.txt: Add clippy + fmt check targets
- Fix all 23 clippy warnings across 6 Rust source files
- Apply cargo fmt to all Rust source files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR comments: switch to Rust validator by default

R6: Removed C_CHECKS/MIGRATED_SCRIPTS scaffolding — all checks are
now built into the compiled validator, no per-check --check flags
needed. The validator runs all checks by default.

R7: add_repo_validation now uses Rust repo_validator_rs by default.
- New option: use_c_repo_validator=ON to fall back to C version
- If cargo is not found, CMake gives a clear FATAL_ERROR with
  msrustup installation instructions and the C fallback option
- All 11 test CMakeLists updated to use REPO_VALIDATOR_EXE/TARGET
  variables set by the parent, so tests work with either validator

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove C validator, keep Rust only

Remove the entire C validator implementation (src/) and the separate
Rust test directory (tests_rust/). The Rust repo_validator_rs is now
the sole compiled validator.

Deleted:
- repo_validation/src/ (16 files, 7,046 lines of C code)
- repo_validation/tests_rust/ (CMakeLists for parallel Rust tests)
- use_c_repo_validator CMake option

Simplified:
- repo_validation/CMakeLists.txt: Rust-only, cargo check with
  clear FATAL_ERROR if not found
- All 11 test CMakeLists: use REPO_VALIDATOR_RS_EXE directly
- tests/CMakeLists.txt: removed C/Rust toggle variables

The C+Rust branch is preserved at feature/c-and-rust-validator
for reference.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Switch to public Rust toolchain

Remove rust-toolchain.toml (was pinned to ms-prod-1.93 internal).
The validator now builds with any Rust toolchain available on PATH.
Updated CMake error message to point to https://rustup.rs/.
Added missing source files to CMake DEPENDS list.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add rust-toolchain.toml with channel = stable

The CI pool has rustup installed but no default toolchain configured.
Setting channel = "stable" tells rustup to use (or auto-install)
the latest stable Rust toolchain.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: use full path for repo_validator_rs executable in CMake

The COMMAND in add_repo_validation used the bare target name
'repo_validator_rs' which only resolves for add_executable()
targets, not add_custom_target(). Use the full REPO_VALIDATOR_RS_EXE
path instead, cached as INTERNAL so it's visible inside the function.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make missing cargo a build-time error, not configure-time

When run_repo_validation=ON but cargo is not found:
- CMake configure succeeds with a WARNING (not FATAL_ERROR)
- All other targets build normally
- Only the repo_validation target fails at build time with
  a clear error message pointing to https://rustup.rs/

When run_repo_validation=OFF (default), cargo is never checked.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Migrate validate_c_build_tools_ref.ps1 to Rust

This migrates the last remaining PowerShell validation script to the
Rust repo_validator_rs tool. The c_build_tools_ref check validates
that pipeline YAML files referencing c-build-tools use either
refs/heads/master or a commit SHA matching the git submodule.

Changes:
- Add checks/c_build_tools_ref.rs implementing the check in Rust
- Add --submodule-sha CLI arg for testing without real git repos
- Register c_build_tools_ref in checks/mod.rs and all_checks()
- Update config.rs with submodule_sha field
- Delete validate_c_build_tools_ref.ps1 (last PS script)
- Remove PowerShell script glob/loop from repo_validation/CMakeLists.txt
- Remove POWERSHELL_EXECUTABLE and VALIDATION_SCRIPTS_DIR from tests
- Convert test CMakeLists to use repo_validator_rs instead of PS
- Add c_build_tools_ref.rs to src_rust/CMakeLists.txt DEPENDS list

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review comments: YAML whitespace and transient comment

R8/R9: c_build_tools_ref.rs - Use flexible whitespace matching for
  YAML repository lines, matching PS1 regex behavior:
  '^\s*-?\s*repository:\s*c_build_tools\s*$'
  Added is_cbt_repository_line() helper using strip_prefix/trim.
  Quick-check also relaxed to just contain "c_build_tools".

R10: tests/CMakeLists.txt - Remove transient word "now" from comment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add detection tests to CTest using WILL_FAIL property

Address PR comment: CTest's set_tests_properties(WILL_FAIL TRUE)
lets us run negative/detection tests that expect exit code 1.
Previously these were only available as manual custom targets.

Added 12 detection tests (one per check) that run the validator
against violation fixtures and expect failure. CTest reports them
as PASSED when the tool correctly exits with code 1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add _failures master targets per check and simplify CTest registration

Each child test CMakeLists.txt now defines a test_validate_<check>_failures
target that depends on the detection sub-targets (which exit with code 1).

The parent tests/CMakeLists.txt CTest section is simplified to a foreach
loop over VALIDATION_CHECKS, registering two tests per check: a passing
test and a WILL_FAIL failures test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix AAA comment parser: skip string/char literals

The find_aaa_positions() scanner was matching // and /* inside
string literals (e.g. " /*" in format strings), causing false
positives. Now tracks string and char literal state the same way
get_function_body() does for brace counting.

Fixes false positive on zrpc tls_security_schannel_helper_ut.c
where a format string containing " /*" was parsed as a block
comment start.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Rust validator to support multi-line SRS tags

The PS validation script uses Singleline regex mode where '.' matches
newlines, allowing SRS tag text to span multiple lines before the
closing **]**. The Rust validator was incorrectly stopping at newlines.

srs_consistency.rs:
- Remove newline break in extract_markdown_srs_tags byte scanner
- Scan across lines up to 4KB to find **]** closing
- Stop at **SRS_ boundaries to avoid crossing into other tags

srs_format.rs:
- When tag line has [** but no **]**, scan subsequent lines
- Stop at new **SRS_ tag definitions to avoid false positives
- Only report missing closing if no **]** found within tag scope

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Detect gratuitous multi-line SRS tags in srs_format check

Flag multi-line tags where the closing **]** is on a separate line but
there is no meaningful content between the opening line and the close.
These should be written as single-line tags instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Split srs_format negative tests into per-violation-type fixtures

Address PR #353 review comment: the single has_violations/ fixture lumped
all violation types into one file, so the WILL_FAIL test passed if even
one type was detected.  A regression breaking detection of a specific type
would be silently masked.

Replace has_violations/ with four fixture directories, each containing
exactly one SRS tag with one violation type:
  - c_comment_close/       ]*/ closing
  - missing_trailing_stars/ **] without trailing **
  - missing_bold_open/     [ without ** opening
  - gratuitous_multiline/  blank line before **]**

Each gets its own detection target; the master target
test_validate_srs_format_failures depends on all four.

Audit of other negative test fixtures:
  - validate_no_tabs            — already 1 type per file (OK)
  - validate_file_endings       — already 1 type per file (OK)
  - validate_enable_mocks       — already 1 type per file (OK)
  - validate_no_vld_include     — already 1 type per file (OK)
  - validate_no_backticks_in_srs — already 1 type per file (OK)
  - validate_test_spec_tags     — already 1 type per file (OK)
  - validate_aaa_comments       — already split by violation type (OK)
  - validate_srs_consistency    — cross-file check, N/A (OK)
  - validate_srs_uniqueness     — cross-file check, N/A (OK)
None of the other checks need the same treatment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Generate help text dynamically from check definitions

The print_usage() function now iterates over all_checks to build
the "Available checks" list using each check's name() and
description() methods. This ensures the help text stays in sync
when checks are added or removed — a new developer only needs to
add their check file and register it in mod.rs.

Also affects --list-checks (already dynamic) and --help output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

## PR Summary: Video Indexer Multi-Modal Enhancements

### Overview
This PR introduces significant enhancements to video processing and image analysis capabilities, focusing on multi-modal AI features and improved metadata handling. **Version updated from 0.233.167 to 0.233.172**.

### 🎯 Key Features

#### 1. **Multi-Modal Vision Analysis for Images**
- Added AI-powered vision analysis for uploaded images using GPT-4 Vision or similar models
- Extracts comprehensive image insights including:
  - AI-generated descriptions
  - Object detection
  - Text extraction from images (OCR)
  - Detailed visual analysis
- New admin setting: `enable_multimodal_vision` to control feature availability
- Vision analysis results stored in document metadata and included in AI Search indexing
- Connection testing endpoint added for vision model validation

#### 2. **Enhanced Document Metadata Citations**
- Implemented metadata-based citations that surface document keywords, abstracts, and vision analysis
- New citation types displayed with distinct visual indicators:
  - **Keywords**: Tagged with `bi-tags` icon, labeled as "Metadata"
  - **Abstract**: Document summaries included as contextual citations
  - **Vision Analysis**: AI-generated image insights labeled as "AI Vision"
- Metadata content passed to AI models as additional context for more informed responses
- Special modal view for metadata citations (separate from standard document citations)

#### 3. **Image Message UI Improvements**
- Enhanced display for user-uploaded images vs AI-generated images
- Added "View Text" button for uploaded images with extracted content or vision analysis
- Collapsible info sections showing:
  - Extracted OCR text from Document Intelligence
  - AI Vision Analysis results
- Proper avatar distinction between uploaded and generated images
- Improved metadata tracking with `is_user_upload` flag

#### 4. **Video Indexer Configuration Updates**
- **BREAKING CHANGE**: Removed API key authentication support
- Now exclusively uses **Managed Identity authentication** for Video Indexer
- Updated admin UI documentation to guide managed identity setup:
  - Enable system-assigned managed identity on App Service
  - Assign "Video Indexer Restricted Viewer" role
  - Configure required ARM settings (subscription ID, resource group, account name)
- Improved validation for required Video Indexer settings
- Enhanced error messaging for missing configuration

#### 5. **Search Scope Improvements**
- Fixed search behavior when `document_scope='all'` to properly include group documents
- Added `active_group_id` to search context when document scope is 'all' and groups are enabled
- Conditional group index searching - only queries group index when `active_group_id` is present
- Prevents unnecessary searches and potential errors when groups aren't in use

#### 6. **Image Context in Conversation History**
- Enhanced conversation history to include rich image context for AI models
- Extracts and includes:
  - OCR text from Document Intelligence (up to max content length)
  - AI Vision analysis (description, objects, text)
  - Structured prompt formatting for multimodal understanding
- **Important**: Base64 image data excluded from conversation history to prevent token overflow
- Only metadata and extracted insights passed to models for efficient token usage

### 🔧 Technical Improvements

#### Backend Changes
- **route_backend_chats.py**: 
  - Added metadata citation extraction logic (~150 lines)
  - Enhanced conversation history building for image uploads
  - Improved search argument handling for group contexts
  
- **functions_documents.py**:
  - New `analyze_image_with_vision_model()` function for AI vision analysis
  - Enhanced `get_document_metadata_for_citations()` integration
  - Vision analysis now runs BEFORE chunk saving to include insights in AI Search indexing
  - Removed redundant blob storage for vision JSON (stored in document metadata)
  
- **route_backend_settings.py**:
  - New `_test_multimodal_vision_connection()` endpoint for testing vision models
  - Supports both APIM and direct Azure OpenAI endpoints
  - Test uses 1x1 pixel sample image for validation

- **functions_search.py**:
  - Added conditional logic for group search execution
  - Prevents empty `active_group_id` from causing search errors

#### Frontend Changes
- **chat-messages.js** (~275 lines changed):
  - Enhanced `appendMessage()` to handle uploaded image metadata
  - New `toggleImageInfo()` functionality for expandable image details
  - Improved citation rendering with metadata type indicators
  - Debug logging for image message processing
  
- **chat-citations.js** (~70 lines added):
  - New `showMetadataModal()` function for displaying keywords/abstracts/vision analysis
  - Enhanced citation click handling to detect metadata citations
  - Separate modal styling and behavior for metadata vs document citations

- **admin_settings.html**:
  - Complete redesign of Video Indexer configuration section
  - Removed all API key references
  - Added managed identity setup instructions with step-by-step guidance
  - Updated configuration display to show resource group and subscription ID

- **_video_indexer_info.html**:
  - Updated modal content to clarify managed identity requirement
  - Added warning banner about authentication type
  - Enhanced configuration display with ARM resource details

### 📊 Files Changed
- **16 files** modified
- **+1,063 insertions**, **-412 deletions**
- Net change: **+651 lines**

### 🧪 Testing Considerations
- Test multi-modal vision analysis with various image types
- Validate metadata citations appear correctly in chat responses
- Verify Video Indexer works with managed identity authentication
- Test search scope behavior with and without groups enabled
- Validate image upload UI shows extracted text and vision analysis
- Confirm conversation history properly handles image context without token overflow

### 🔐 Security & Performance
- Managed identity authentication improves security posture (no stored API keys)
- Image base64 data excluded from conversation history prevents token exhaustion
- Metadata citations add minimal overhead while providing rich context
- Vision analysis runs efficiently during document processing pipeline

### 📝 Configuration Required
Admins must configure:
1. Enable `enable_multimodal_vision` in admin settings
2. Select vision-capable model (e.g., `gpt-4o`, `gpt-4-vision-preview`)
3. For Video Indexer: Configure managed identity and ARM resource details
4. Enable `enable_extract_meta_data` to surface metadata citations

---

This PR significantly enhances the application's multi-modal capabilities, providing users with richer context from images and documents while maintaining efficient token usage and robust security practices.

* Conversation Management Features (#532)

New Features
1. Pin Conversations
Users can pin important conversations to keep them at the top of the list
Pinned conversations display a pin icon (📌) in the conversation header and details modal
Pin icon appears before the conversation title
Bulk pin/unpin operations available in multi-select mode
Pinned conversations always appear first, sorted by most recent activity
2. Hide Conversations
Users can hide conversations to declutter their workspace without deleting them
Hidden conversations display an eye-slash icon (👁️‍🗨️) in the conversation header and details modal
Eye-slash icon appears next to the pin icon (if pinned)
Bulk hide/unhide operations available in multi-select mode
Toggle visibility of hidden conversations using the eye icon in the sidebar
3. Two-Tier Conversation Search
Quick Search (Sidebar)
Instant title-based filtering of conversations
Search icon in sidebar activates inline search input
Real-time filtering as you type
Clear button to reset search
Expand button to open advanced search modal
Advanced Search (Modal)
Full-text search across all message content
Multiple filter options:
Date range (from/to)
Chat type (personal/group/public)
Classifications (multi-select)
Has uploaded files
Has generated images
Pagination (20 results per page)
Message snippets with highlighted search terms (50 chars before/after match)
Click to navigate directly to specific messages
Search history tracking (last 20 searches)
Clickable search history to repeat searches
4. Message Highlighting & Navigation
Search results highlight matched text in yellow (amber in dark mode)
Smooth scroll animation to navigate to specific messages
Pulse animation draws attention to the target message
Highlights persist for 30 seconds before auto-clearing
Works across conversation switches
5. Multi-Select Mode
Select multiple conversations for bulk operations
Visual checkboxes appear when entering selection mode
Bulk actions available:
Pin/unpin selected conversations
Hide/unhide selected conversations
Delete selected conversations
Selection mode accessible from conversation dropdown menu
Auto-exit after 30 seconds of inactivity
6. Enhanced Conversation Details Modal
Displays pin icon if conversation is pinned
Displays eye-slash icon if conversation is hidden
Shows both icons at the top of the modal (next to title)
Status section shows visual badges for pinned/hidden state
Comprehensive metadata display

## PR Summary: Video Indexer Multi-Modal Enhancements

### Overview
This PR introduces significant enhancements to video processing and image analysis capabilities, focusing on multi-modal AI features and improved metadata handling. **Version updated from 0.233.167 to 0.233.172**.

### 🎯 Key Features

#### 1. **Multi-Modal Vision Analysis for Images**
- Added AI-powered vision analysis for uploaded images using GPT-4 Vision or similar models
- Extracts comprehensive image insights including:
  - AI-generated descriptions
  - Object detection
  - Text extraction from images (OCR)
  - Detailed visual analysis
- New admin setting: `enable_multimodal_vision` to control feature availability
- Vision analysis results stored in document metadata and included in AI Search indexing
- Connection testing endpoint added for vision model validation

#### 2. **Enhanced Document Metadata Citations**
- Implemented metadata-based citations that surface document keywords, abstracts, and vision analysis
- New citation types displayed with distinct visual indicators:
  - **Keywords**: Tagged with `bi-tags` icon, labeled as "Metadata"
  - **Abstract**: Document summaries included as contextual citations
  - **Vision Analysis**: AI-generated image insights labeled as "AI Vision"
- Metadata content passed to AI models as additional context for more informed responses
- Special modal view for metadata citations (separate from standard document citations)

#### 3. **Image Message UI Improvements**
- Enhanced display for user-uploaded images vs AI-generated images
- Added "View Text" button for uploaded images with extracted content or vision analysis
- Collapsible info sections showing:
  - Extracted OCR text from Document Intelligence
  - AI Vision Analysis results
- Proper avatar distinction between uploaded and generated images
- Improved metadata tracking with `is_user_upload` flag

#### 4. **Video Indexer Configuration Updates**
- **BREAKING CHANGE**: Removed API key authentication support
- Now exclusively uses **Managed Identity authentication** for Video Indexer
- Updated admin UI documentation to guide managed identity setup:
  - Enable system-assigned managed identity on App Service
  - Assign "Video Indexer Restricted Viewer" role
  - Configure required ARM settings (subscription ID, resource group, account name)
- Improved validation for required Video Indexer settings
- Enhanced error messaging for missing configuration

#### 5. **Search Scope Improvements**
- Fixed search behavior when `document_scope='all'` to properly include group documents
- Added `active_group_id` to search context when document scope is 'all' and groups are enabled
- Conditional group index searching - only queries group index when `active_group_id` is present
- Prevents unnecessary searches and potential errors when groups aren't in use

#### 6. **Image Context in Conversation History**
- Enhanced conversation history to include rich image context for AI models
- Extracts and includes:
  - OCR text from Document Intelligence (up to max content length)
  - AI Vision analysis (description, objects, text)
  - Structured prompt formatting for multimodal understanding
- **Important**: Base64 image data excluded from conversation history to prevent token overflow
- Only metadata and extracted insights passed to models for efficient token usage

### 🔧 Technical Improvements

#### Backend Changes
- **route_backend_chats.py**: 
  - Added metadata citation extraction logic (~150 lines)
  - Enhanced conversation history building for image uploads
  - Improved search argument handling for group contexts
  
- **functions_documents.py**:
  - New `analyze_image_with_vision_model()` function for AI vision analysis
  - Enhanced `get_document_metadata_for_citations()` integration
  - Vision analysis now runs BEFORE chunk saving to include insights in AI Search indexing
  - Removed redundant blob storage for vision JSON (stored in document metadata)
  
- **route_backend_settings.py**:
  - New `_test_multimodal_vision_connection()` endpoint for testing vision models
  - Supports both APIM and direct Azure OpenAI endpoints
  - Test uses 1x1 pixel sample image for validation

- **functions_search.py**:
  - Added conditional logic for group search execution
  - Prevents empty `active_group_id` from causing search errors

#### Frontend Changes
- **chat-messages.js** (~275 lines changed):
  - Enhanced `appendMessage()` to handle uploaded image metadata
  - New `toggleImageInfo()` functionality for expandable image details
  - Improved citation rendering with metadata type indicators
  - Debug logging for image message processing
  
- **chat-citations.js** (~70 lines added):
  - New `showMetadataModal()` function for displaying keywords/abstracts/vision analysis
  - Enhanced citation click handling to detect metadata citations
  - Separate modal styling and behavior for metadata vs document citations

- **admin_settings.html**:
  - Complete redesign of Video Indexer configuration section
  - Removed all API key references
  - Added managed identity setup instructions with step-by-step guidance
  - Updated configuration display to show resource group and subscription ID

- **_video_indexer_info.html**:
  - Updated modal content to clarify managed identity requirement
  - Added warning banner about authentication type
  - Enhanced configuration display with ARM resource details

### 📊 Files Changed
- **16 files** modified
- **+1,063 insertions**, **-412 deletions**
- Net change: **+651 lines**

### 🧪 Testing Considerations
- Test multi-modal vision analysis with various image types
- Validate metadata citations appear correctly in chat responses
- Verify Video Indexer works with managed identity authentication
- Test search scope behavior with and without groups enabled
- Validate image upload UI shows extracted text and vision analysis
- Confirm conversation history properly handles image context without token overflow

### 🔐 Security & Performance
- Managed identity authentication improves security posture (no stored API keys)
- Image base64 data excluded from conversation history prevents token exhaustion
- Metadata citations add minimal overhead while providing rich context
- Vision analysis runs efficiently during document processing pipeline

### 📝 Configuration Required
Admins must configure:
1. Enable `enable_multimodal_vision` in admin settings
2. Select vision-capable model (e.g., `gpt-4o`, `gpt-4-vision-preview`)
3. For Video Indexer: Configure managed identity and ARM resource details
4. Enable `enable_extract_meta_data` to surface metadata citations

This PR significantly enhances the application's multi-modal capabilities, providing users with richer context from images and documents while maintaining efficient token usage and robust security practices.

* Conversation Management Features (#532)

New Features
1. Pin Conversations
Users can pin important conversations to keep them at the top of the list
Pinned conversations display a pin icon (📌) in the conversation header and details modal
Pin icon appears before the conversation title
Bulk pin/unpin operations available in multi-select mode
Pinned conversations always appear first, sorted by most recent activity
2. Hide Conversations
Users can hide conversations to declutter their workspace without deleting them
Hidden conversations display an eye-slash icon (👁️‍🗨️) in the conversation header and details modal
Eye-slash icon appears next to the pin icon (if pinned)
Bulk hide/unhide operations available in multi-select mode
Toggle visibility of hidden conversations using the eye icon in the sidebar
3. Two-Tier Conversation Search
Quick Search (Sidebar)
Instant title-based filtering of conversations
Search icon in sidebar activates inline search input
Real-time filtering as you type
Clear button to reset search
Expand button to open advanced search modal
Advanced Search (Modal)
Full-text search across all message content
Multiple filter options:
Date range (from/to)
Chat type (personal/group/public)
Classifications (multi-select)
Has uploaded files
Has generated images
Pagination (20 results per page)
Message snippets with highlighted search terms (50 chars before/after match)
Click to navigate directly to specific messages
Search history tracking (last 20 searches)
Clickable search history to repeat searches
4. Message Highlighting & Navigation
Search results highlight matched text in yellow (amber in dark mode)
Smooth scroll animation to navigate to specific messages
Pulse animation draws attention to the target message
Highlights persist for 30 seconds before auto-clearing
Works across conversation switches
5. Multi-Select Mode
Select multiple conversations for bulk operations
Visual checkboxes appear when entering selection mode
Bulk actions available:
Pin/unpin selected conversations
Hide/unhide selected conversations
Delete selected conversations
Selection mode accessible from conversation dropdown menu
Auto-exit after 30 seconds of inactivity
6. Enhanced Conversation Details Modal
Displays pin icon if conversation is pinned
Displays eye-slash icon if conversation is hidden
Shows both icons at the top of the modal (next to title)
Status section shows visual badges for pinned/hidden state
Comprehensive metadata display

feat(types,serialize,codegen): add AssignTargetShape per-span signedness (PR B) (#824)

* feat(types,serialize,codegen): add AssignTargetShape per-span signedness (PR B)

- Add AssignTargetShape { is_unsigned: bool } to TypeCheckOutput alongside
  AssignTargetKind; populated in check_stmt after target type synthesis.
- Add Ty::is_unsigned() predicate matching U8/U16/U32/U64.
- Serialize assign_target_shapes array in msgpack/json; bump schema v4 -> v5.
- C++ ast_types.h and msgpack_reader.cpp parse the new field.
- generateAssignStmt calls requireAssignTargetShapeOf (fail-closed);
  eliminates all isUnsigned = false / resolvedTypeOf fallback patterns.
- Focused tests: checker shape population, serializer round-trips, C++ reader.

* fix(astgen): add AssignTargetShape generator and regenerate msgpack_reader.cpp

AssignTargetShapeEntry was manually written into msgpack_reader.cpp in
the original PR commit but never wired into the hew-astgen generator,
causing the generated_reader_matches_checked_in_file snapshot test to
fail on CI.  Additionally, the committed file used an older multi-line
formatting style instead of the current compact one-liner format emitted
by the generator.

Changes:
- Add assign_target_shape_entry_parser() to hew-astgen/src/special_cases.rs
- Wire it into codegen.rs after the AssignTargetKind parsers
- Add assign_target_shapes to the program_parser in special_cases.rs
- Regenerate msgpack_reader.cpp from the updated generator

* fix(codegen): check identifier authority mismatch before shape lookup

The two fail-closed tests
  identifier_local_assignment_kind_mismatch_fails_closed
  identifier_actor_field_assignment_kind_mismatch_fails_closed
were failing because requireAssignTargetShapeOf fired before the
LocalVar/ActorField authority mismatch guards, emitting a spurious
'missing assign_target_shapes' error instead of the expected mismatch
diagnostic.

Add early-exit checks for ActorField (currentActorName empty) and
LocalVar (no local binding) immediately after the kind lookup, before
the shape lookup.  The downstream duplicate checks are unreachable in
the mismatch case and unchanged.

* test(codegen): populate assign_target_shapes in authority-mismatch test helpers

The makeIdentifierAssignmentAuthorityProgram and
makeActorFieldAssignmentAuthorityProgram helpers built programs at
schema v4 without assign_target_shapes.  After PR B's schema bump to v5
and the fail-closed shape lookup in generateAssignStmt, these helpers
need a matching AssignTargetShapeEntry (is_unsigned=false) per the
target span, and schema_version=5.

This makes the test programs consistent with what the real checker
produces, so the authority-mismatch tests exercise the full mismatch-
detection path rather than relying solely on the early-exit guards added
in fd2741a.

commit.message:
fix: escape ADO parameter expressions to prevent Backstage Nunjucks mangling

Backstage's template engine uses ${{ }} as its Nunjucks delimiter.
All ${{ parameters.X }} expressions in the skeleton were being interpreted
by Backstage and replaced with empty string (undefined variable), causing
the pipeline to always fail the guard check.

Fix:
- Wrap all env: block parameter references with {% raw %}...{% endraw %}
  so Backstage passes them through to the generated file unchanged
- Guard checks now test $CROSSPLANE_SP_CLIENT_SECRET / $ARGOCD_ADO_PAT
  (env vars already mapped in the same step's env: block) instead of
  the compile-time ${{ parameters.X }} expression

commit.message:
Make all retention/cohort data mathematically consistent

All 5 cohorted datasets now derive from the same assumptions:
- User retention: 100->78->68->...->47% at M11 (~50% churn)
- Revenue retention: 100->108->115->...->146% (moderate expansion)
- Total COGS: declines as users churn (0.9->0.64 for May cohort)
- Rev/user = (rev_retention / user_retention) * M0_RPU -> ~3.1x by M11
- COGS/user = derived from total_cogs / remaining_users -> ~1.5x by M11
- Newer cohorts: higher M0 rev (bigger), lower M0 COGS/user (efficiency)

commit.message:
fix(step1): remove sidecar inject annotation from VMI template; add DEBUG runbook

The sidecar.istio.io/inject: "true" annotation on the VMI pod template
was causing the OSSM webhook to inject istio-proxy in step1, where the
namespace intentionally has no istio-injection=enabled label.

The Istio mutating webhook fires on EITHER the namespace label OR the pod
annotation. With the annotation present, the sidecar was injected even
without the namespace label. The injected istio-proxy container failed its
readiness probe on port 15021 (connection refused) — the classic KubeVirt
masquerade + Envoy iptables conflict (D-008). This left the pod at 1/2,
made the endpoint not ready, and caused the Route to return 503.

Fix:
- Remove sidecar.istio.io/inject: "true" from vm.yaml VMI template.
  Sidecar injection for step2 is driven solely by the namespace label
  applied in deploy/step2-ossm/mesh-config/namespace-patch.yaml.
- Keep traffic.sidecar.istio.io/kubevirtInterfaces: k6t-eth0 — it is a
  no-op in step1 and required in step2 to prevent Envoy iptables rules
  from conflicting with KubeVirt masquerade on k6t-eth0.
- Add deploy/step1-vm/DEBUG.md — step-by-step oc runbook for diagnosing
  503 errors, unexpected sidecar injection, cloud-init timing issues,
  and step2 sidecar readiness.

To apply on a live cluster:
  oc apply -k deploy/step1-vm/
  oc delete vmi eshoplite-vm -n eshoplite-vm
  # KubeVirt recreates VMI; new pod will be 1/1 → endpoints ready → Route works

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix extension lifecycle event handlers being silently dropped (#7562)

* Fix extension lifecycle event handlers being silently dropped

Replace fmt.Sprintf-based handler dedup in EventDispatcher with ID-based
registration and context-scoped lifecycle management. The old dedup compared
closure code pointers, which are shared across closures from the same function
literal, causing only one extension's handler to survive when multiple
extensions subscribe to the same event.

- EventDispatcher: unique ID per registration, context-based cleanup, stale
handler skip in RaiseEvent
- Inline hooks: fresh temp script per execution via PrepareExecutionPath
- Service hooks: signature-based idempotent registration on ServiceConfig
- Workflow steps: scoped child contexts with defer cancel for handler cleanup
- Config reloads: CopyRuntimeStateTo preserves dispatchers and hook state

fix: switch pipeline triggering from variables to templateParameters

Variables sent via the runs API require allowOverride:true in the definition
AND explicit env: mapping on every step that reads them in bash. This made
the ARGOCD_ADO_PAT check always fail because the guard step had no env entry.

ADO templateParameters are resolved at YAML compile-time and are the correct
way to pass values into a parameterized YAML pipeline via the API.

Changes:
- Remove definitionVariables block from pipeline definition creation — no
  need to pre-register parameter slots; they are declared in the YAML
- Run body: use templateParameters: { KEY: value } instead of
  variables: { KEY: { value, isSecret } }
- Remove unused redactVariablesForLog function
- Update logging to reflect parameters (not variables) are being sent
- Input schema field 'variables' kept unchanged so template.yaml needs no edits

fix: switch secrets from queue-time variables to ADO runtime parameters

Queue-time variables require an explicit env: mapping on every step that uses
them in bash. The Azure Login guard was missing ARGOCD_ADO_PAT in its env:,
causing the check to always fail even when the value was correctly sent.

ADO templateParameters are the right solution: declared in the YAML parameters:
block and sent via templateParameters in the runs API, they are resolved at
YAML compile-time via ${{ parameters.X }} and are available everywhere without
per-step env: mappings.

Changes:
- Add parameters: block at top of pipeline YAML (CROSSPLANE_SP_CLIENT_SECRET
  and ARGOCD_ADO_PAT, both with default: '')
- Replace all $(CROSSPLANE_SP_CLIENT_SECRET) → ${{ parameters.CROSSPLANE_SP_CLIENT_SECRET }}
- Replace all $(ARGOCD_ADO_PAT) → ${{ parameters.ARGOCD_ADO_PAT }}
- Guard checks now use compile-time ${{ parameters.X }} expansion in bash
  so they work correctly regardless of env: mappings
- Update header comment

commit.message:
fix: improve schedule running-status accuracy and matching safety (#531)

* ci(release): separate staging vs production Docker tags

- Always publish env tag (staging/production) for lrauv-dash-5 images
- Add staging-only versioned tag (staging-{{version}}) for rollback/debugging
- Restrict semver tags ({{version}}, {{major}}.{{minor}}, {{major}}) to production runs only
- Keep latest tag production-only
- Drop branch ref tag to avoid ambiguous/shared tags between environments

* docs(runbook): update deployment runbook for staging/production tag separation

- Add "What Changed" section explaining the overlapping Docker tag issue
  and the fix applied on 2026-03-09
- Document new developer workflow for staging bumps and production releases
- Fix stale Docker image references (was :5/:latest, now :staging/:production)
- Fix Docker Compose example to show both containers with correct Watchtower labels
- Update manual update commands to target specific services, not both at once

* fix(#516, #488): correct schedule status and Started/Ended label in ScheduleSection

Issue #488: status prop was hardcoded to 'pending' for all real (non-TBD) statuses,
so cancelled/completed/running missions all rendered with a pending icon. Now passes
the actual status from the API through as ScheduleCellStatus.

Issue #516: description label checked !!unixTime (always truthy) instead of
mission.status to decide "Started" vs "Ended", causing active/running missions to
display "Ended" in Schedule History. Now uses ['pending','running'].includes(status).

* fix(#522): address Copilot review — runtime status validation and unixTime guard

- Replace 'as ScheduleCellStatus' type assertion with toScheduleCellStatus()
  helper that whitelists known values and falls back to 'completed', preventing
  a runtime error if the backend returns an unexpected status string.
- Guard description against missing unixTime: instead of falling back to epoch
  (DateTime.fromMillis(0)), return undefined when unixTime is absent so no
  misleading timestamp is shown.

* Initial plan

* fix: show loading state during session refresh to prevent Unavailable flash on browser refresh

* fix(#514): show full doc name and docType in DocsSection

DocCell was truncating document names to 3 words, making documents
with the same short name (e.g. three separate "Ahi-42 - MBTS" docs)
indistinguishable. Two changes:

- Remove the 3-word truncation in DocCell; show the full label instead,
  with CSS truncation as overflow safety and a title tooltip for names
  that overflow the available width.
- Add an optional `secondary` prop to DocCell and render it as a small
  muted label below the document name (e.g. "form", "normal").
- Pass item.docType as secondary from DocsSection so users can see the
  document type (FORM, NORMAL, TEMPLATE, FILLED) beneath each title.

* fix: use empty string fallback for description when unixTime absent (TypeScript strict)

* feat(#523): derive real running/ended status from /events/mission-started

The /deployments/commandStatus API always returns status:'TBD' so the
Schedule History previously showed every mission as 'completed'/'Ended'.

The /events/mission-started endpoint returns all missions in newest-first
order regardless of how they were triggered (operator command or
MissionManager auto-trigger like Default). The most recent entry is the
currently running mission; all prior entries have ended.

Changes:
- Add useMissionStartedEvent call (limit 50) to ScheduleSection
- Build missionStatusMap from the sequence: index 0 = 'running', rest =
  'completed'
- Enrich each TBD command/run event with the derived status by matching
  mission names extracted from the event data string
- Inject the currently running mission at the top if it was auto-triggered
  (not found in operator command log) — fixes the Default mission gap from
  issue #523
- Add missionNameFromStartedText and missionNameFromEventData helpers
  (exported for testability)

* feat(#523): show Started HH:mm (X ago) / Ended: HH:mm (or TBD) per mission

Replaces the flat missionStatusMap with a timeline that pairs each
mission-started event with its real end time (= the start time of the
next mission in the sequence). The most recent entry (index 0) is still
running so its endedAt remains undefined → shows 'Ended: TBD'.

Changes:
- Add endedAt?: number to CommandStatusItem
- Build missionTimeline from /events/mission-started (newest-first):
  endedAt = events[i-1].unixTime for completed, undefined for running
- Match run/command events to timeline entries using closest-startedAt
  within a 5-minute window to handle repeated same-name missions
- Update ScheduleCell description to 'Started 2:01 (12 min ago)'
- Update ScheduleCell description2 to 'Ended: TBD' or 'Ended: 2:47'
- Increase refetchInterval to 60s for live running→ended transitions

* feat: use 'Run' prefix for non-mission commands, 'Started' for missions

Mission events (eventType='run' or load+run pattern) show 'Started 2:01'.
Plain commands (stop, restart logs, configSet, etc.) show 'Run 2:01'.

* feat: add end times disclaimer under Schedule History header

* feat: use faPersonRunning icon for running mission status

* fix: prevent Leaflet _leaflet_pos crash on Overview and deployment open

fitBounds triggers a CSS zoom animation. When React re-renders during
that animation, Leaflet _onZoomTransitionEnd reads _leaflet_pos from a
pane element that has moved — TypeError crash caught by Next.js overlay.

The try/catch around fitBounds calls does not help because the crash
occurs inside Leaflet async animation callback after fitBounds returns.
Only animate:false prevents the animation from starting.

Added animate:false to all three fitBounds call sites in VehiclePath
(initial deployment load, grouped overview map retries) and
WaypointPreviewPath — fixing both the Overview and per-vehicle crashes.

* fix: pin Mission Log filter bar above all missions for consistent layout

Previously the filter bar appeared between running and historic missions,
so its position shifted depending on how many running missions were found.
Ahi (with a matched running mission) showed it partway down; Triton (no
running mission) showed it at the top — inconsistent across vehicles.

Now the filter bar always sits immediately after the Schedule is running
banner, running missions appear below it alongside historic ones, and the
indexOffset is uniform for all cells. Also renamed the section header
from 'Schedule History' to 'Mission Log' since it now encompasses
currently running missions too.

* revert: restore 'Schedule History' section label

* feat: use 'Ran' prefix for non-mission commands

* fix(#514): remove truncation, show full doc name with docType secondary label

- Remove 3-word truncation that was hiding distinguishing parts of names
- Add secondary?: string prop to DocCellProps
- Render secondary (docType) as small muted label below the document name
- Add title={label} tooltip so full name is visible on hover
- DocsSection already passes secondary={item?.docType} on this branch

* fix(#514): capitalize first letter of each word in doc name label

* chore: revert api-proxy target back to okeanids for PR review

* fix(#522): address Copilot review — simplify status mapping and use != null for unixTime guard

- toScheduleCellStatus already maps TBD→completed for unknown values,
  so the explicit TBD ternary was redundant; removed for clarity
- Changed unixTime truthy check to != null so a theoretical 0 epoch
  value does not silently produce an empty description

* test(#514): add DocCell tests for secondary prop, title attr, empty cases

Addresses Copilot review on PR #528:
- secondary renders with correct text when provided
- secondary absent (undefined) produces no secondary element
- secondary empty string produces no secondary element
- label button has title attribute matching full label for tooltip regression

* fix(#522): add refetchInterval to SupportedQueryOptions to fix CI build

TypeScript strict build rejected refetchInterval on useMissionStartedEvent
call because SupportedQueryOptions did not declare the property.
refetchInterval is a standard React Query option — adding it here.

* fix(#522): address second Copilot review pass

- Move fitRef/fit.current update inside try block so a failed fitBounds
  does not prevent a later retry for the same bounds (VehiclePath +
  WaypointPreviewPath)
- Check both event.data and event.text when detecting if the currently
  running mission is already in the command log
- Use eventType:'run' and data??text for injected auto-triggered missions
  so parseMissionCommand/isMissionCommand classify them correctly
- Guard DateTime.toRelative() null return so description never shows
  empty parentheses like '()'
- Extract missionNameFromStartedText/missionNameFromEventData to
  lib/missionUtils.ts and add 11 unit tests in jest/missionUtils.test.ts

* test(#514): fix brittle DocCell tests per Copilot review

- 'secondary is undefined' now queries for the element itself rather
  than asserting a hard-coded string that was never in the render
- 'secondary is empty string' compares full container HTML against a
  baseline render instead of relying on a Tailwind CSS class name query

* fix(#522): address third Copilot review pass

- Use currentRawEvent.text (not .data which does not exist on
  GetMissionStartedEventResponse) for injected mission event.data
- Derive scheduleStatus from most recent sched pause/resume command
  rather than missions[0] which may now be the injected synthetic entry
- Change !item.event.unixTime to == null so a valid 0-epoch timestamp
  does not accidentally skip timeline enrichment

* fix(#528): address third Copilot review pass

- Add data-testid='doc-cell-secondary' to secondary span so tests query
  by stable identity rather than a Tailwind CSS class name
- Update DocCell tests to use getByTestId/queryByTestId — no brittle
  class selectors or innerHTML comparisons

* fix(#522): address fourth Copilot review pass

- WaypointPreviewPath: reset fit.current when route becomes empty so
  re-selecting the same waypoint set correctly re-triggers fitBounds
  instead of being skipped (fit.current === routeAsString)
- Synthetic mission injection: construct data as 'load <name>.tl;run'
  so parseMissionCommand produces a consistent label and the
  'Use for new mission' modal receives a valid parseable path

* fix(#528): address fourth Copilot review pass

- Move toLowerCase() from DocCell to DocsSection call site so the
  component renders secondary as-provided without imposing casing rules
- DocsSection now passes item?.docType?.toLowerCase() to DocCell

* feat(#522): add ~ prefix to derived end times in Schedule History

* fix(#515): fully resolve browser refresh logging user out of PIC/On-Call session

Root causes discovered and fixed beyond the original Copilot PR #527:

1. Stale api-client dist — source edits were silently ignored at runtime because
   Next.js resolved @mbari/api-client via the exports field (dist/) not main
   (src/). All subsequent fixes required a package rebuild to take effect.

2. SSR hydration cookie gap — react-use-cookie initialises via useState() on the
   server where document.cookie is unavailable, returning '' on every refresh.
   useSessionToken now owns the state itself and syncs from getCookie() in a
   useEffect() that only runs on the client after hydration.

3. Cookie wiped on tokenless refresh response — the real okeanids /user/token
   endpoint returns 200 with user profile data but no token field. The previous
   onSettled handler resolved to setSessionToken('') and erased the cookie.
   Both useRefreshSessionToken and useCreateLogin now use onSuccess with an
   explicit if (data?.token) guard so the cookie is never cleared accidentally.

4. currentUser not restored after browser refresh — the useEffect that hydrates
   currentUser required refreshedSession.data.token to be present. When the
   server omits the token the user was authenticated (cookie valid) but currentUser
   stayed null. The effect now falls back to the existing sessionToken from the
   cookie, and decodes the JWT payload to recover firstName/lastName/email/roles
   so the user avatar shows real initials instead of "uu".

5. Session expiry not surfaced — when the server rejects an expired token (401)
   during a background refresh the previous code left the user in a broken
   "authenticated" state with stale data. A new useEffect now calls logout()
   when refreshedSession.isError is true and the user held an active session,
   ensuring the login prompt is shown cleanly.

6. Leaflet _controlCorners crash — GoogleMutant fires async Google API callbacks
   after the Leaflet map is removed. Patched map.fire() on each instance to be
   a no-op once _controlCorners is null, preventing the unhandled TypeError.

Tests added:
- TethysApiProvider: tokenless refresh restores auth; cookie not wiped; token
  expiry shows login prompt (20 tests total, all passing)
- useRefreshSessionToken: setSessionToken called only when response has token;
  not called on 401 (5 tests total, all passing)
- useSessionToken (new jest/useSessionToken.test.ts): SSR empty init; cookie
  read after mount; write round-trip; absent cookie; logout case (5 tests)

* fix: use 24-hour time format (H:mm) in Schedule History

* fix: improve schedule running-status accuracy

Improve mission status resolution in Schedule History by normalizing mission names, supporting load commands without file extensions, and preferring interval-based mission-started matching so active missions reliably show as running.

* fix: harden mission matching to avoid false running statuses

Use path-aware mission matching when both sides include paths, fall back to basename-only matching when needed, and skip running-row promotion when the current mission key cannot be parsed.

* fix: align schedule history filtering with displayed status

Use normalized schedule cell status during history filtering so rows displayed as completed are included when the Completed filter is selected.

* fix: address Copilot review edge cases across auth and schedule

Harden waypoint coordinate normalization, decode JWT profile payloads using base64url rules, keep session token Secure checks SSR-safe, and move mission key matching helper out of component scope.

* fix: handle waypoint focus index zero correctly

Use explicit numeric/null checks for focusedWaypointIndex in DeploymentMap so waypoint index 0 behaves correctly, and remove unused mission utility re-exports from ScheduleSection.

* fix: only show schedule history header when history exists

Derive the Schedule History header/filter visibility from presence of non-running, non-pending rows so the history section only appears when there are actual historical entries.

* fix(#531): use missionPathFromEventData in handleDuplicate for extensionless load paths

isMissionCommand was updated to accept extensionless load paths but
handleDuplicate still used an extension-only regex, causing the 'Use
for new mission' modal to open with an empty mission value for those
commands. Replace inline regex with the already-imported
missionPathFromEventData which handles both cases.

* fix(#531): address Copilot review — widen mission path regex and fix Schedule History heading position

- Widen withExtension regex in missionNameFromEventData/missionPathFromEventData
  from [A-Za-z0-9_/] to [A-Za-z0-9_/.-] so mission paths containing dashes or
  dots (e.g. Long-Range/mbari-echo-5.25.tl) are extracted correctly instead of
  silently falling through to the extensionless fallback.
- Add test cases for dashed/dotted mission paths with extensions.
- Fix indexOfPastSchedule: was always staticHeaderCellOffset, causing the
  Schedule History heading to render above pending/running items. Now computed
  as staticHeaderCellOffset + scheduledCells.length so it appears at the
  boundary between live and historical entries.
- Adjust indexOffset in cellAtIndex to correctly map virtual indices to
  results[] for both scheduled (before heading) and historical (after heading).

* fix(#531): show precise minutes in Started relative time instead of rounding to hours

Replace Luxon toRelative() which switches to hours at 45+ min with a custom
formatter: <60 min shows 'X min. ago', 60+ min shows 'Xh Ym ago' (e.g. '1h 25m ago')
so the elapsed time is always accurate to the minute.

* fix(#531): remove unused missionNameFromEventData import from ScheduleSection

* fix(#531): address Copilot review — raw mission path for modal and fix routeAsString

- Add rawMissionPathFromEventData to missionUtils: extracts mission path
  preserving original case and extension (e.g. Science/profile_station.tl)
  for use as MissionModal meta.mission pre-fill. missionPathFromEventData
  (normalized/lowercase) is reserved for internal matching only.
- Use rawMissionPathFromEventData in handleDuplicate so the 'Use for new
  mission' modal auto-selects the correct entry from the mission list.
- Fix WaypointPreviewPath routeAsString: was calling .flat().join() on an
  array of {lat,lon} objects, producing '[object Object],...' for every
  route. Now serializes as 'lat,lon|lat,lon|...' so route changes with
  different coordinates are correctly detected.
- Add tests for rawMissionPathFromEventData covering case/extension
  preservation and extensionless fallback.

* fix: use JWT short-form role claim in AuthController

ClaimTypes.Role (full URI) was written verbatim into the JWT payload when
constructing JwtSecurityToken directly — the OutboundClaimTypeMap is not
applied in this path. MAUI AuthService raw-JSON decoder looks for the key
'role', so CurrentUser.Roles was always empty and the Crew role gate always
denied login.

Change new Claim(ClaimTypes.Role, r) to new Claim('role', r) to emit the
JWT-standard short form explicitly. AuthService.ParseJwtClaims already reads
'role' correctly — no MAUI changes needed.
